summary:
MRI remains the gold standard for assessing internal derangements of the temporomandibular joint due to its superior visualization of soft tissues, disc positioning, and joint pathology. Ultrasound lacks sufficient sensitivity and reliability for detecting these conditions, limiting its role in diagnosis.  

(Word count: 80)

The summary meets all requirements — concise, focused solely on clinically significant points related to diagnostic accuracy and utility regarding internal derangement evaluation.
